title: Isolepis brevicaulis
taxonomy: Isolepis brevicaulis is classified within the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family consists of a large number of species, many of which are found in wetland environments. The genus Isolepis belongs to the tribe Cariceae, which is a group of sedges that are typically found in aquatic or wet environments. The classification of Isolepis brevicaulis is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, the division Magnoliophyta, and the class Liliopsida. This classification system is used to organize and categorize plants according to their evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Isolepis brevicaulis, also known as short-stemmed club rush, is a perennial aquatic plant belonging to the sedge family (Cyperaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Isolepis brevicaulis include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Isolepis brevicaulis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as dried plant material for use in capsules and supplements.
